description: Reconstruct the May 29, 1919 total solar eclipse from Arthur Eddington's expedition site on Príncipe island (1.66° N, 7.40° E) at 14:15 UTC to reveal the background stars used to test General Relativity. The view should replicate an astrometric plate analysis with the atmosphere and ground hidden, and star labels and constellation lines visible. Capture a screenshot of the eclipsed Sun and surrounding stars in Taurus to ~/Pictures/stellarium/. Write an expedition summary report to ~/Desktop/eddington_eclipse.txt including the year 1919, the location Principe, the constellation Taurus, and the name of at least one prominent star or cluster visible near the Sun.
